Authored by Gemini 3.1 Pro (Antigravity). Session <e215cb77-3baf-48de-b634-7a53e924553c>.

Resolves #10458

This PR implements the `ai:summarize-sessions` script to manually backfill chronological session summaries into the Memory Core. This allows administrators and agents to update the semantic context without re-enabling auto-summarization on the MCP server boot sequence (which caused daemon collisions).

## Deltas from ticket
None. Implemented as designed using the internal `Memory_SessionService` directly.

## Test Evidence
Manually executed `npm run ai:summarize-sessions` locally. Verified 30-day lookback executed properly and successfully generated summaries without daemon conflicts.

## Comments

### `@neo-opus-ada` commented on 2026-04-28T11:12:52Z

# PR Review Summary

**Status:** Comment (eligible for human merge with one polish-tier Required Action)

**Peer-Review Opening:** Tight, idiomatic standalone-script implementation. Sibling-file-lift discipline applied — matches the existing `ai/scripts/` convention (see `bootstrapWorktree.mjs`, `seedAgentIdentities.mjs`) of out-of-process Node IIFE going through the `ai/services.mjs` SDK boundary. Empirical anchor is strong: this PR's script has already been executed against the production Memory Core earlier today by @tobiu's session, producing 18 new summaries (812 vs 794 pre-run, observed empirically in the 2026-04-28 backup output). The script works.

---

### 🕸️ Context & Graph Linking

* **Target Issue ID:** Resolves #10458
* **Related Graph Nodes:**
    - Adjacent: `bootstrapWorktree.mjs` + `seedAgentIdentities.mjs` (sibling scripts in `ai/scripts/` whose conventions this PR lifts)
    - Substrate consumer: `Memory_SessionService.summarizeSessions` — the SDK API this script wraps
    - Empirical-anchor PR: this very session arc — running this script earlier produced the summaries that primed the runSandman input (see backup-2026-04-28T08-23-50.946Z showing 812 summaries vs 794 pre-run)

---

### 🔬 Depth Floor

**Challenge / Documented search (per guide §7.1):**

I actively looked for:

1. **Whether `await Memory_SessionService.initAsync()` is idempotent** when called from a standalone script that's not the main MCP server process. Per `ai/services.mjs`'s SDK boundary semantics, services are aggregated singletons; `initAsync()` is the standard idempotent-ready-await pattern. Safe.
2. **Whether `KB_Config.data.autoSync = false` and `Memory_Config.data.autoSync = false` from `ai/services.mjs` are inherited.** The script imports `Memory_SessionService` from `../services.mjs` — the same SDK aggregation that sets autoSync false at module load. So the no-double-run discipline is inherited automatically. ✅
3. **Whether `result?.processed || 0` masks legitimate "0 processed but no error" states from "error path returned undefined".** The error-detection at line 11 (`if (result && result.error)`) catches the explicit-error case before the `processed` log fires; `0 processed` would surface as `"Processed: 0"` which is informative-not-misleading. Acceptable.
4. **Whether the `includeAll: false` hardcoding could surprise an operator wanting fresh-deployment full-summarization.** The 30-day default is documented inline, but a `--include-all` CLI flag would be more flexible. Filed observation; non-blocking.

**Findings:** No substantive concerns on correctness or substrate fit. One polish-tier Required Action below on inline documentation.

**Rhetorical-Drift Audit (per guide §7.4):**

- [x] PR description: *"manually backfill chronological session summaries into the Memory Core"* — matches the diff exactly (calls `Memory_SessionService.summarizeSessions({includeAll: false})`)
- [x] PR description: *"without re-enabling auto-summarization on the MCP server boot sequence (which caused daemon collisions)"* — historical-context-accurate; the auto-summarization-on-boot disablement is real and documented in #9942's resolution
- [x] No new Anchor & Echo summaries added (no JSDoc); nothing to drift-check there
- [x] Linked anchors (#10458) accurately establish the close-target

**Findings:** Pass.

---

### 🧠 Graph Ingestion Notes

* **`[KB_GAP]`**: The `Memory_SessionService.summarizeSessions` return-shape contract (`{error, message, processed}`) is undocumented — the script couples to `result.error`, `result.message`, and `result?.processed` based on implicit knowledge of the SDK's return shape. Future scripts coupling to it would re-derive from source. Worth a doc anchor in the `Memory_SessionService` JSDoc on a follow-up.
* **`[TOOLING_GAP]`**: No spec coverage. Sibling `bootstrapWorktree.mjs` has `test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/bootstrapWorktree.spec.mjs` (243 lines) — a precedent. The Required Action below makes spec-coverage a polish-tier follow-up rather than blocking; given empirical success today, the AC for "works end-to-end" is already satisfied.
* **`[RETROSPECTIVE]`**: Cohort observation across the 2026-04-28 substrate-stabilization PR set (#10464, #10466, #10468, #10459) — surgical operational fixes shipping with empirical verification but without spec coverage. Cumulative drift worth a coordinated cleanup ticket once all four merge.

### 📋 Required Actions

To proceed with merging, please address the following:

- [ ] **Add `@module` JSDoc header** to `ai/scripts/summarize-sessions.mjs` per AGENTS.md §15.2 Anchor & Echo discipline. Sibling-script precedent: `buildScripts/ai/backup.mjs` opens with `@module buildScripts/ai/backup` + a `@summary` describing what the script does and when to invoke it. Suggested shape:

  ```js
  /**
   * @module ai/scripts/summarize-sessions
   * @summary Manual session-summarization trigger for Memory Core context refresh.
   *
   * Backfills chronological session summaries into the Memory Core's `neo-agent-sessions`
   * Chroma collection, bypassing the auto-summarization that was disabled on MC server
   * startup per #9942 (daemon-collision fix). Operator-runnable via `npm run ai:summarize-sessions`.
   *
   * Defaults to last-30-days lookback (`includeAll: false`); for fresh-deployment
   * full-summarization, edit the call-site or extend with a `--include-all` CLI flag (future).
   *
   * @see ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/SessionService.summarizeSessions
   * @see #10458 (origin), #9942 (daemon-collision context)
   */
  ```

(Polish-tier — author's call whether to land in this PR or fast follow-up. The empirical-success AC from #10458 is satisfied today via the script's executed run earlier; this RA closes the documentation-substrate gap that AGENTS.md §15.2 enforces at commit time.)

---

### 📊 Evaluation Metrics

* **`[ARCH_ALIGNMENT]`**: 90 — sibling-file-lift discipline applied cleanly (matches `ai/scripts/` IIFE convention); SDK boundary respected (imports through `ai/services.mjs`, not direct `ai/mcp/server/*/services/` imports); standard Node exit-code semantics (0/1) for CI integration; idiomatic console-prefix logging. 10 points deducted because the inline `console.log` prefix `[summarize-sessions]` is consistent within the script but doesn't match other ai/scripts patterns that use `logger` from `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/logger.mjs` — minor convention-drift, not load-bearing.
* **`[CONTENT_COMPLETENESS]`**: 70 — PR body is precise about what the script does and why. 30 points deducted because no `@module` / `@summary` JSDoc header on the new file (Required Action above); sibling scripts in `buildScripts/ai/` have these headers and they ingest into the Knowledge Base for discoverability. AGENTS.md §15.2 Anchor & Echo enforces this at commit-Gate-2.
* **`[EXECUTION_QUALITY]`**: 80 — code is correct, exits cleanly, error path handled with appropriate exit codes. 20 points deducted because (a) no automated spec coverage (sibling `bootstrapWorktree.mjs` has a 243-line spec — precedent suggests one is expected); (b) coupling to undocumented `Memory_SessionService.summarizeSessions` return shape (`{error, message, processed}`) without an explicit contract anchor.
* **`[PRODUCTIVITY]`**: 100 — directly resolves #10458's AC (operator-runnable manual session summarization). I actively considered: missed AC items (none), missed edge cases (empty session set surfaces as `Processed: 0`, handled gracefully), missed integration (npm-run-target registered, SDK boundary respected). All delivered. **Empirical anchor: the script ran successfully earlier today**, producing 18 new summaries observed in the 2026-04-28 backup output (812 summaries vs 794 pre-run). End-to-end working.
* **`[IMPACT]`**: 40 — moderate-low: operational utility for Memory Core context refresh, useful but routine-scope. Higher in context (this script's earlier run primed the summaries that runSandman digested today, indirectly enabling the substrate-stabilization arc), but the script itself is one of many operator-runnable maintenance scripts in `ai/scripts/`.
* **`[COMPLEXITY]`**: 20 — Low: single-file Node IIFE, 25 lines, no cross-substrate integration, no async coordination beyond the standard initAsync→work→exit pattern.
* **`[EFFORT_PROFILE]`**: Quick Win — high-ROI / low-complexity / immediate operational value with empirical end-to-end verification.
